commonMedications.name: Semaglutide / GLP-1 (Ozempic/Wegovy) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D covers for diabetes (tier 3). $25 copay typical. Without coverage ~$900/mo. IRA negotiated price TBD.

commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1-2; $4-15 copay

commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D tier 2; $10-25 copay. IRA cap applies.

commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

medicine.pharmacyAccess: Excellent. CVS, Walgreens, Walmart, ShopRite, Wegmans pharmacies throughout Cherry Hill/South Jersey area. Close proximity to Philadelphia hospital pharmacies. Mail-order through Part D plans.
medicine.prescriptionSystem: Medicare Part D prescription drug plan. Choose standalone PDP or Medicare Advantage with drug coverage. Annual out-of-pocket cap $2,000 under Inflation Reduction Act. NJ PAAD program for additional prescription assistance.
medicine.insuranceCoverage: Medicare Part D covers most prescriptions. New Jersey's PAAD (Pharmaceutical Assistance to the Aged and Disabled) provides additional prescription coverage for NJ seniors with income under $28,769 (single) or $35,270 (couple).

annualExamCost.notes: Original Medicare does NOT cover routine vision exams. Must have separate vision plan or Medicare Advantage with vision.

vision.insuranceCoverage: Need separate vision plan or Medicare Advantage with vision. Standalone vision plans (VSP, EyeMed) run $15-25/month. Access to Philadelphia's Wills Eye Hospital via PATCO for specialist care.
vision.specialistWaitTime: 1-2 weeks for routine eye exams in Cherry Hill area. Excellent access to Philadelphia ophthalmology (Wills Eye Hospital, Penn) via PATCO. Local optometrists widely available.
vision.seniorConsiderations: Medicare covers cataract surgery and glaucoma screening. Annual glaucoma screening covered for high-risk beneficiaries. Diabetic retinopathy screening covered. PATCO provides easy access to world-class Philly eye care.

annualCleaningCost.notes: Original Medicare does NOT cover routine dental. Need separate dental plan or Medicare Advantage with dental.

dental.insuranceCoverage: Need separate dental plan ($20-50/month) or Medicare Advantage with dental. Standalone plans (Delta Dental of New Jersey, Cigna, Guardian) widely available. Penn Dental in Philly accessible via PATCO for lower-cost care.
dental.dentistAvailability: Excellent in Cherry Hill/South Jersey suburbs. Many dental practices, short wait times (1-2 weeks). Penn Dental Medicine accessible via PATCO for reduced-cost dental care.

categories.name: Dining Out
categories.seniorDiscounts: AARP restaurant discounts. Cherry Hill Mall area dining options.
categories.notes: Lunch for 2 at moderate restaurant ~$33 + 6.625% tax + 20% tip = ~$42/meal, 2x/week = ~$345/mo

categories.name: Amazon Prime
categories.seniorDiscounts: Medicaid recipients qualify for Prime at $6.99/mo.

categories.name: Gym/Fitness
categories.seniorDiscounts: SilverSneakers free with many Medicare Advantage plans.

categories.name: Parks/Outdoors
categories.seniorDiscounts: NJ state parks. Cooper River Park free.

categories.name: Coffee/Cafes
categories.seniorDiscounts: Senior coffee discounts at major chains.

monthlyPass.notes: PATCO Hi-Speedline monthly pass varies by zone ($58-90 regular). NJ Transit bus senior fare half-price. Seniors 62+ get reduced PATCO fare.

publicTransit.coverage: PATCO Hi-Speedline connects Cherry Hill (Woodcrest/Haddonfield stations) to downtown Philadelphia in 20-25 minutes. NJ Transit buses serve Cherry Hill area. Car still needed for most local errands in suburbs.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: PATCO offers reduced fares for seniors 62+ with Senior Citizen reduced fare ID. NJ Transit offers half-fare for seniors 62+ on buses. SEPTA Senior Key Card honored for free rides when crossing into Philly.

rideShare.availability: Good. Uber and Lyft available in Cherry Hill area. May have slightly longer waits than Philadelphia proper.
rideShare.notes: GoGoGrandparent available. Camden County offers Access Link paratransit for eligible disabled/senior residents. Taxi services available.

registration.notes: NJ registration $35-84/year depending on vehicle weight. Title fee $60. No personal property tax on vehicles in NJ. NJ does not charge sales tax on used cars from private sellers (under certain conditions).

insurance.notes: New Jersey has some of the highest car insurance rates in the US. No-fault state. Cherry Hill suburban rates lower than urban NJ but still above national average. Senior safe driver discounts 5-15%.

fuel.notes: ~$3.35/gallon average in NJ. NJ gas tax $0.424/gallon. NJ is one of two states (with Oregon) where attendants pump gas. Typical senior drives 8,000-12,000 miles/year.

parking.notes: Free parking at most Cherry Hill shopping centers, restaurants, and residential areas. Suburban parking is generally free. PATCO station parking free at Woodcrest, $1/day at Haddonfield.

tolls.notes: NJ Turnpike and Atlantic City Expressway tolls if traveling. Ben Franklin Bridge $5 (eastbound to NJ only — free westbound to Philly). PATCO avoids bridge toll. E-ZPass recommended.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: NJ Mature Driver discount for completing AARP Smart Driver course (5-15% insurance reduction). Camden County Senior Services offers transportation assistance for qualifying seniors.

transportation.walkability: Low. Cherry Hill is a typical NJ suburb — car-dependent for most errands. Haddonfield downtown is walkable. PATCO stations provide walkable transit hubs. Voorhees Town Center moderately walkable.

cycling.infrastructure: Limited. Some bike lanes on main roads. Cooper River Park trail for recreation. NJ Bike & Walk Coalition advocating for improvements. Suburban road design not optimized for cycling.
cycling.notes: No formal bike share program in Cherry Hill. Cooper River Park and cross-county trail system provide recreational options. Camden County improving bike infrastructure.

electricVehicle.purchaseNotes: Tesla Model 3 ~$35K, Model Y ~$45K, Hyundai Ioniq 5 ~$42K, Chevy Equinox EV ~$28K. Federal tax credit up to $7,500 (income limits apply). Some states add incentives.
electricVehicle.incentiveNotes: Federal EV tax credit up to $7,500 under IRA (income < $150K single / $300K joint). Must be new vehicle, MSRP < $55K sedan / $80K SUV. Used EV credit up to $4,000.

chargingHome.notes: Level 2 (240V) home charger. Electricity ~$0.12-0.16/kWh. ~1,000 mi/month = ~300 kWh = $36-48/mo.

chargingPublic.notes: Tesla Supercharger ~$0.40-0.50/kWh. Electrify America ~$0.43/kWh. ChargePoint varies. Free L2 chargers at some retailers.

insurance.notes: EV insurance 15-25% higher than comparable ICE vehicle. Shop Liberty Mutual, Progressive, Tesla Insurance where available.

maintenance.notes: No oil changes, fewer brake jobs (regen braking). Tire rotation every 7,500 mi. Cabin air filter annually. Brake fluid every 2-3 yrs. 12V battery every 4-5 yrs (~$100). Tires wear faster on heavier EVs.

registration.notes: Many states add EV-specific fees ($100-275/yr) to replace lost gas tax revenue. Virginia: $64/yr EV fee.

electricVehicle.batteryWarranty: 8 years / 100,000 miles (federal minimum). Most manufacturers cover 70-80% capacity retention.
electricVehicle.depreciationNotes: EVs depreciate 40-50% in first 3 years, though Tesla holds value better. Used EV market improving. Budget ~$3,000-5,000/yr depreciation on $35K vehicle.
electricVehicle.totalMonthlyNotes: Includes: charging ($50), insurance ($180), maintenance ($50/mo avg), registration ($17/mo). Excludes: purchase/loan payment, depreciation.

discounts.name: Medicare
discounts.description: Federal health insurance at 65. Part A (hospital) premium-free for most. Part B (medical) ~$185/month. Part D (drugs) $10-50/month. Access to Philadelphia hospital systems (Penn, Jefferson) via PATCO.

discounts.name: AARP Membership
discounts.description: Annual membership $16. Discounts on insurance, dining, travel, retail, and entertainment. Available at age 50+.

discounts.name: NJ Social Security Tax Exemption
discounts.description: New Jersey fully exempts Social Security benefits from state income tax. NJ also excludes up to $100,000 of retirement income for couples with gross income under $150,000.

discounts.name: NJ No Sales Tax on Groceries/Clothing
discounts.description: New Jersey charges no sales tax on groceries, clothing, or shoes. Significant ongoing savings compared to states that tax these essentials.

discounts.name: PATCO/NJ Transit Senior Fares
discounts.description: Reduced PATCO fares for seniors 62+. NJ Transit half-fare on buses for 62+. SEPTA Senior Key Card honored for free rides in Philadelphia.

discounts.name: NJ PAAD Prescription Assistance
discounts.description: NJ Pharmaceutical Assistance to the Aged and Disabled covers prescription copays ($6-9) for seniors 65+ with income under $28,769 (single) or $35,270 (couple).

discounts.name: Camden County Senior Services
discounts.description: Transportation assistance, Meals on Wheels, senior centers, health screenings, legal services, and SHIP Medicare counseling.

discounts.name: SilverSneakers Fitness
discounts.description: Free gym membership through participating Medicare Advantage and Medigap plans. Access to 17,000+ locations nationwide.

plans.name: T-Mobile 55+ Essentials
plans.notes: 2 lines unlimited talk/text/data. NJ wireless tax ~14% + surcharges. 5G included.

breakdown.notes: Water, sewage, and trash are separate utility bills — see utilities breakdown.

housing.taxNotes: New Jersey has no residential rental tax. NJ has highest property taxes in the US (avg 2.23%) but this is landlord responsibility. Cherry Hill/Camden County slightly below state average.
housing.leaseTerms: 12-month standard lease. Security deposit max 1.5 months rent (~$3,450). NJ tenant protections are strong.
housing.marketNotes: South Jersey suburban market with good availability of 2BR houses. More affordable than neighboring Philadelphia suburbs on the PA side.

items.name: Chicken (whole/thighs)
items.notes: Primary protein for both adults & dog

items.name: Ground Turkey / Beef
items.notes: Lean ground for dog food base

items.name: Organ Meat (liver/heart)
items.notes: Essential for balanced home-cooked dog diet

healthcare.coverageSummary: Medicare Part A (free) + Part B ($174.70/mo pp) + Medigap Plan G (~$200/mo pp) + Part D (~$45/mo pp). IRA out-of-pocket cap $2,000/yr starting 2025.

costs.notes: Higher due to AMD + multiple conditions. Age-rated.
Medicare covers 80%, Medigap covers 20% coinsurance. ~6-8 injections/yr at $40-50 copay each.
OCT scans every 4-8 weeks. Medicare + Medigap covers most.
Quarterly bloodwork/visits. Low copay with Medigap.
Annual visit + as-needed. Mild asthma, infrequent.
Slightly lower — no AMD, but diabetes management
All generics — lower tier plan sufficient
Quarterly A1C, bloodwork. Covered well by Medicare.
Medicare Part B covers glucose monitors. Some copays.

shared.notes: Separate dental plan ~$35/person. Medicare does not cover dental.
Basic vision plan. AMD specialist visits covered by Medicare Part B.
Covered 100% by Medicare — no copay for preventive.

blackHairCare.label: Black Hair Care
blackHairCare.notes: Camden County and nearby Philadelphia provide excellent Black hair care options.

providers.name: Duafe Holistic Hair Care
providers.notes: Top-rated natural hair salon. Worth the trip across the bridge. By appointment.

providers.name: Styles by Sav
providers.notes: Local salon experienced with textured hair, braids, color, silk press.

whiteHairCare.label: White/Caucasian Hair Care
whiteHairCare.notes: Cherry Hill has many well-established salons along Haddonfield Road and Route 70.

providers.name: Pistachio Hair Studio
providers.notes: Highly rated local salon. Color specialists, grey blending, balayage.

providers.name: Salon Bella Vita
providers.notes: Aveda concept salon. Gentle color products. Experienced with mature hair.

mensBigTall.label: Men's Big & Tall Clothing
mensBigTall.notes: DXL in Cherry Hill area. Close proximity to King of Prussia for Nordstrom extended sizes.

providers.name: DXL Big + Tall
providers.notes: Convenient local big & tall store. Full size range.

providers.name: Macy's (Cherry Hill Mall)
providers.notes: Extended sizes section. Men's big & tall department in-store.

womensClothing.label: Women's Regular Clothing
womensClothing.notes: Cherry Hill Mall is the primary shopping destination. Moorestown Mall nearby for additional options.

providers.name: Macy's (Cherry Hill Mall)
providers.notes: Full women's regular size range. Good sales events.

providers.name: LOFT / White House Black Market
providers.notes: Regular sizes. Work and casual styles. Frequent promotions.

shoes.label: Shoes
shoes.notes: Good shoe options at Cherry Hill Mall and nearby Route 70 retail corridor.

providers.name: DSW (Cherry Hill)
providers.notes: Wide widths available. Good variety of comfort and fashion brands.

providers.name: Nordstrom Rack (Cherry Hill)
providers.notes: Discounted designer shoes. Extended sizes available.
